sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: enVVeno Medical Announces Reverse Stock Split
feed.summary: enVVeno Medical (NASDAQ:NVNO) announced a 1-for-35 reverse stock split, effective January 20, 2026, to regain compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements. This move, approved by stockholders, is part of a broader strategy for 2026 aimed at creating shareholder value and advancing the company's position in treating deep venous disease. The split will reduce outstanding shares and adjust equity awards and warrants accordingly.

feed.title: Why Pfizer Inc. (PFE) is One of the Best Affordable Stocks Under $30
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) has shown positive results from its BREAKWATER trial for metastatic colorectal cancer, with a 64% objective response rate for its BRAFTOVI regimen, significantly outperforming standard care. Despite these clinical successes, the stock faces analyst skepticism due to upcoming patent expirations, with Berenberg Bank and UBS reaffirming "Hold" and "Neutral" ratings, respectively, and setting a $25 price target. The company is actively pursuing new strategies, such as the MTSR obesity deal, to counteract potential revenue losses.

feed.title: What Does Wall Street Think About ServiceNow (NOW)?
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts have recently adjusted their price targets for ServiceNow (NOW). Stifel and Wells Fargo both cut their targets, while maintaining a "Buy" or "Overweight" rating, respectively, highlighting the potential of AI in driving future performance. ServiceNow is set to release its Q4 and full-year fiscal results on January 28.

feed.title: Where is Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) Headed According to Analysts?
feed.summary: Jefferies has reaffirmed a Buy rating on Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) with a $1,300.00 price target, following positive Phase 3b trial results for its Taltz and Zepbound combination in psoriatic arthritis and obesity. Additionally, Eli Lilly announced an agreement to acquire Ventyx Biosciences for approximately $1.2 billion, enhancing its portfolio of therapies for inflammatory-mediated diseases. The article notes LLY's strong position in pharmaceuticals but suggests that some AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk.

feed.title: This is What The Street Thinks About Uber Technologies (UBER)
feed.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) has received mixed but generally positive analyst ratings, with Mizuho Securities reaffirming a Buy and Jefferies and BofA also maintaining Buy ratings, while Cantor Fitzgerald slightly cut its price target but kept an Overweight rating. Analysts highlight the potential for durable mobility growth, progress in AV partnerships, and the positive impact of AI on the internet sector. The company also recently showcased its production-intent robotaxi vehicles at CES 2026, with autonomous testing already underway.

feed.title: SEC Dismisses Proceeding Against IEH Corporation
feed.summary: IEH Corporation announced that the SEC has dismissed the Division of Enforcement's proceeding seeking revocation of the registration of IEHC's securities. President and CEO Dave Offerman expressed satisfaction, noting the company has rectified past filing delinquencies and plans to uplist its stock for greater liquidity and investment opportunities. The company, known for its high-performance connectors, looks forward to future growth after this positive development.

feed.title: Barclays Maintains Overweight Rating on Energy Transfer LP (ET)
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained its Overweight rating on Energy Transfer LP (ET) despite cutting its price target to $22 from $25. The firm acknowledges concerns about re-contracting events but notes the company's commercial discipline and constructive forward outlook. This decision follows Energy Transfer LP's announcement of its 2026 capital investment outlook, which includes significant growth capital for its natural gas network and projected Adjusted EBITDA.

feed.title: Pfizer Advances Prazosin Manufacturing Shift With New Bioequivalence Study
feed.summary: Pfizer is conducting a Phase 1 bioequivalence study for prazosin hydrochloride to qualify a manufacturing site transfer from Barceloneta, Puerto Rico, to Ascoli, Italy. This open-label, randomized, crossover study aims to confirm that the drug manufactured at the new site has the same absorption, clearance, and safety profile as the current product. The successful completion of this study will ensure supply chain flexibility and cost efficiency for prazosin, a medication used to treat high blood pressure and heart failure, without impacting patient outcomes.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Reveals Points Choice and It Could Change Loyalty Forever
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has officially announced its new "Points Choice" loyalty program, set to take effect on January 30, 2026. This program allows guests to apply loyalty points earned from Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea Cruises to any of the three brands, enhancing flexibility and choice for members. Unlike competitor Carnival Cruise Line's recent loyalty program changes, Points Choice is expected to be well-received due to its user-friendly, cross-brand point application system.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Makes a Big Change to its Loyalty Program… And Passengers Will Love It
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has introduced a new "Points Choice" feature for its loyalty program, allowing passengers to transfer points earned on one of its cruise lines (Royal Caribbean, Celebrity, Silversea) to another. This change offers greater flexibility for guests, enabling them to consolidate loyalty points on their preferred line even if they sail on different brands within the group. The points transfer comes with specific conditions, including a 14-day post-sailing limit, applicability only to cruises departing on or after January 30, 2026, and fixed exchange rates.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for TransDigm Group (TDG) to $1804, Reit
feed.summary: UBS analyst Gavin Parsons has reiterated a Buy rating for TransDigm Group (TDG) and raised the price target to $1804, an increase of 0.33% from the previous target. Other analysts have also recently adjusted their price targets for TDG, reflecting dynamic market expectations for the specialized aerospace parts manufacturer. The average one-year price target from 20 analysts is $1,593.71, implying an 11.53% upside from the current share price, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $1611.51.

feed.title: Is Verizon back up? How customers impacted by outage get $20 credit
feed.summary: Verizon experienced a nationwide outage on January 14, affecting over 1.5 million customers for about 10 hours. The company has apologized and is offering a $20 credit to all impacted customers, which can be claimed through the myVerizon app. While the outage has been resolved, users still experiencing issues are advised to restart their devices.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share for $329
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a ten percent owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), recently purchased a share of common stock for $329.91. This transaction follows the company's third-quarter 2025 earnings which missed expectations, a strategic partnership with Bolt Data & Energy, and the announcement of a three-for-one stock split. KeyBanc initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, highlighting the company's land and royalty interests in Texas.

feed.title: March 20th Options Now Available For Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) with a March 20th expiration date are now trading. For investors interested in selling cash-secured puts, a $155 strike price offers a potential 0.65% return (3.68% annualized) if the option expires worthless, representing a 12% discount to the current trading price. Conversely, for those considering covered calls, an $180 strike price could yield a 7.35% return if the stock is called away, with a 50% chance of the call expiring worthless and yielding a 4.79% boost (27.31% annualized).

feed.title: John Hancock Financial Opportunities Fund (NYSE:BTO) Short Interest Up 236.8% in December
feed.summary: Short interest in John Hancock Financial Opportunities Fund (NYSE: BTO) surged by 236.8% in December, reaching 4,031 shares, though the days-to-cover ratio remains low at 0.1 days. The fund declared a quarterly dividend of $0.65 per share, equating to a 7.2% yield. Institutional ownership stands at 19.55%, with shares trading near $35.88.

feed.title: DOG: An ETF That Profits From Declines In The Dow Jones Industrials (NYSEARCA:DOG)
feed.summary: The ProShares Short Dow30 ETF (DOG) is designed to profit from declines in the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) without using leverage. While the DJIA has shown relative resilience, the author advises a "Hold" rating for DOG currently. The author anticipates a future opportunity to buy DOG when the broader equity market faces a significant downturn.

feed.title: Union Pacific tells MARS Norfolk Southern merger will lower costs by reducing handoffs
feed.summary: Union Pacific met with customers at the winter meeting of the Midwest Association of Rail Shippers (MARS) to discuss its proposed merger with Norfolk Southern. The company states the merger will lower costs by reducing handoffs and using faster, more efficient, and price-competitive routes. The statement emphasizes that single-line, coast-to-coast service will be more cost-efficient, benefiting US businesses and consumers.

feed.title: Nepsis Inc. Increases CAVA Stake to 176,694 Shares with $2.94 Million Acquisition
feed.summary: Nepsis Inc. has significantly increased its stake in CAVA, acquiring an additional 52,776 shares for approximately $2.94 million, bringing its total holdings to 176,694 shares. This move signals Nepsis's confidence in CAVA's long-term growth potential and its commitment to expand to over 1,000 restaurants by 2032, despite the stock's recent underperformance. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for CAVA, with an average 1-year price target of $71.69.

feed.title: BlackRock Earnings: Market Gains and Record Inflows Drive AUM and Revenue to New Highs
feed.summary: BlackRock reported record assets under management (AUM) of $14.042 trillion at the end of December 2025, driven by strong market gains and record inflows, especially into its iShares ETF platform. Morningstar analysts expect to increase their fair value estimate for BlackRock stock from $1,170 to $1,200 per share, viewing the shares as slightly undervalued despite anticipating potential headwinds from governmental policies. The firm's organic AUM growth rate of 8.6% for the fourth quarter significantly outperformed expectations and its peers.

feed.title: Raymond James To Acquire $46B Asset Manager Clark Capital
feed.summary: Raymond James Investment Management, a subsidiary of Raymond James, has announced its plan to acquire Clark Capital Management Group, a Philadelphia-based asset manager with $46 billion in assets. This acquisition marks the seventh firm to join Raymond James’ multi-boutique investment management division. Clark Capital will maintain its brand and leadership team, offering multi-asset class investments, model portfolios, and mutual funds, with the deal expected to close in Q3 2026.

feed.title: NCL opens sales for Norwegian Aura, its largest ship to date
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line has commenced sales for the Norwegian Aura, its largest ship to date, which is set to debut in May 2027. This newest addition to the Prima Class will feature expansive family-focused amenities including a large open-air activity complex called Ocean Heights with five waterslides and an expanded Ocean Boulevard. The ship will homeport in Miami starting June 2027, offering seven-day Caribbean itineraries after its European debut.

feed.title: Union Pacific Defends Merger with Norfolk Southern Amid Customer Support
feed.summary: Union Pacific is actively defending its proposed merger with Norfolk Southern, engaging with customers and addressing misinformation to highlight the benefits of creating America's first transcontinental railroad. The company asserts that the merger will enhance competition, improve network resilience through a 50,000-route mile system, and has submitted an application to the Surface Transportation Board with 2,000 letters of support. Despite this, analyst views on Norfolk Southern (NSC) vary, with several recent downgrades citing regulatory risks and ongoing softness in freight demand.

feed.title: AtriCure Inc (ATRC) Shares Up 8.49% on Jan 15
feed.summary: AtriCure Inc (ATRC) saw its shares rise 8.49% to $40.51 on January 15, placing it 6.18% below its 52-week high. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $52.78, suggesting a 30.28% upside, and an "Outperform" consensus rating. GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $47.32, indicating a 16.81% upside for the stock.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages Dick’s Sporting Goods, Inc. Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Discuss Their Rights
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating whether certain officers and directors of Dick’s Sporting Goods, Inc. (NYSE: DKS) breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages current, long-term shareholders to contact them to discuss legal options, including corporate governance reforms, return of funds to the company, or financial incentives. Halper Sadeh LLC offers contingent fee arrangements, emphasizing that shareholder involvement can improve company policies and enhance shareholder value.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) is predicted to beat earnings estimates for the quarter ended December 2025, with a positive Earnings ESP of +5.94% and a Zacks Rank #3. Analysts expect a quarterly EPS of $0.28, a 9.7% year-over-year decline, and revenues of $5.05 billion, down 11.7%. The company has a history of beating EPS estimates, exceeding them three out of the last four quarters.

feed.title: Applied Materials jumps after Barclays lifts rating on AI-driven spending outlook
feed.summary: Barclays upgraded Applied Materials (AMAT) stock to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing expectations of increased capital expenditure driven by AI-related demand from memory and logic chipmakers. The firm raised its price target to $220, predicting robust spending trends through 2025 as the industry invests in new fabs and high-bandwidth memory for AI. This positive outlook led to a significant jump in Applied Materials' stock price.

feed.title: Sell GE HealthCare Stock, UBS Says. The Risks Aren’t Known.
feed.summary: UBS has issued a bearish call on GE HealthCare Technologies, advising investors to sell the stock due to unknown risks. This recommendation led to a decline in GE HealthCare's share price on Thursday. The article highlights the uncertainty surrounding the company's future performance.

feed.title: New Everus board picked as MDU Resources readies construction spinoff
feed.summary: MDU Resources has announced the anticipated board of directors for Everus Construction Group, its construction services subsidiary set to spin off into an independent, publicly traded company by late 2024. The new board will comprise seven directors, including six independent members, with Dale S. Rosenthal expected to chair the board. This spinoff aims to optimize shareholder value and transform MDU Resources into a pure-play regulated energy delivery business.

feed.title: BlackRock tops fourth-quarter forecasts as assets climb to $14 trillion
feed.summary: This article states that BlackRock exceeded its fourth-quarter forecasts, with its assets under management reaching $14 trillion. It covers the company's financial performance.

feed.title: Franklin Resources stock hits 52-week high at 26.08 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Franklin Resources (BEN) stock reached a 52-week high of $26.08, reflecting a 38.34% increase over the past year. The company boasts a market capitalization of $13.57 billion, a 5.15% dividend yield, and a 45-year history of consistent dividend payments, with InvestingPro data suggesting it may still be undervalued. Recent positive developments include exceeding AUM forecasts, a reiterated Buy rating from TD Cowen, and an increased quarterly dividend.

feed.title: ETFs Primed to Benefit From America's $2.7 Billion Nuclear Push
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Energy has awarded $2.7 billion to three companies to boost domestic uranium enrichment capacity, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign suppliers and revitalize the nuclear industry. This initiative supports a "nuclear renaissance" by securing fuel for existing and advanced reactors, creating a favorable environment for domestic miners, next-gen reactor developers, and power generators. Investors can capitalize on this trend through nuclear and uranium-focused ETFs, which offer diversified exposure to the entire value chain and mitigate the high volatility associated with individual stocks.

feed.title: Cisco Stock Rises 8% in 3 Months: Will the Rally Continue in 2026?
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) stock has risen 7.7% in the past three months, outperforming its peers due to its aggressive AI push and expanding security footprint. The company projects significant AI infrastructure revenues and strong networking growth, supported by partnerships with NVIDIA. Cisco's positive guidance for Q2 and fiscal 2026, driven by its expanding portfolio and AI initiatives, suggests continued strong performance and makes it a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) stock.

feed.title: Verizon users report service outage affecting thousands
feed.summary: Thousands of Verizon users are reporting a service outage. The issues appear to be widespread, affecting various services provided by Verizon.

feed.title: Susquehanna Adjusts TransDigm Group PT to $1,500 From $1,450, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Susquehanna has increased its price target for TransDigm Group (TDG) to $1,500 from $1,450, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook on the aerospace components manufacturer. Other analysts have also recently adjusted their price targets for TransDigm, indicating ongoing analyst attention to the company's valuation.

feed.title: Transdigm Group (NYSE:TDG) Price Target Raised to $1,804.00
feed.summary: UBS Group has raised its price target for Transdigm Group (NYSE:TDG) to $1,804.00, maintaining a "buy" rating, which suggests a 26.26% upside. This follows the aerospace company beating quarterly EPS estimates and increasing its revenue by 11.5% year-over-year. The article also notes significant insider selling, with 187,238 shares valued at approximately $252.6 million offloaded in the last 90 days.

feed.title: Raymond James buys $46B Philadelphia wealth manager
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has acquired Clark Capital Management, a Philadelphia-based wealth management firm. Clark Capital Management has grown significantly over the past decade, increasing its assets under management from $3 billion to over $46 billion. This acquisition further expands Raymond James' presence in the wealth management sector.

feed.title: Medtronic inks deal to build brain computer interface into surgical system
feed.summary: Medtronic has partnered with Precision Neuroscience to integrate the latter's Layer 7 cortical interface with Medtronic's StealthStation surgical navigation system. This collaboration aims to enhance neurosurgery by providing surgeons with real-time functional and structural brain information, accelerating the clinical adoption of advanced brain-computer interface technology. The deal leverages Medtronic's established platform to bring Precision Neuroscience's FDA-cleared microelectrode array to widespread use.

feed.title: Omnissa Ranks Highest Across All Four Use Cases in the Gartner® Critical Capabilities for Endpoint Management Tools Report
feed.summary: Omnissa has been recognized as a Leader in the inaugural 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) Tools and achieved the highest ranking across all four Use Cases in the Gartner Critical Capabilities for Endpoint Management Tools report. The company attributes this recognition to its Omnissa Workspace ONE UEM platform, which provides a unified, AI-driven approach to managing and securing endpoints across diverse environments. Omnissa's Workspace ONE UEM scored 4.95 out of 5.0 in the Autonomous Endpoint Management Use Case and achieved the highest scores in all four evaluated use cases.

feed.title: Senator wants answers from BNY on $378M of Epstein transfers (BK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Senator Ron Wyden is seeking information from BNY Mellon regarding $378 million in transfers from Jeffrey Epstein's accounts, as part of an investigation into the financing of Epstein's sex-trafficking activities. The inquiry raises concerns about BNY Mellon's adherence to anti-money laundering laws and its vigilance in monitoring suspicious transactions. BNY Mellon denies Epstein was a direct client and is providing documentation while defending against what it calls "meritless allegations."

feed.title: Sempra sets Oct. 15 payout dates for common and preferred dividends
feed.summary: Sempra (NYSE: SRE) has announced that its board of directors declared quarterly common and semi-annual preferred stock dividends. The common dividend is $0.62 per share and the preferred Series C dividend is $24.375 per share, both payable on October 15, 2024. Record dates for shareholders to receive these dividends are September 26, 2024, for common stock and October 1, 2024, for Series C preferred stock.

feed.title: Keysight Unveils Machine Learning Toolkit to Accelerate Device Modeling and PDK Development
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies has released a new Machine Learning Toolkit within its Device Modeling Software Suite, aiming to significantly reduce the time required for device modeling and parameter extraction from weeks to hours. This AI/ML-driven solution is designed to accelerate Process Design Kit (PDK) delivery and Design Technology Co-Optimization (DTCO) processes, addressing the increasing complexity of advanced semiconductor technologies. The toolkit features an ML optimizer and auto-extraction flows, enabling faster and more accurate model development across various device types.

feed.title: UBS Adjusts Price Target on CACI International to $752 From $759, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: UBS has adjusted its price target for CACI International, reducing it slightly from $759 to $752, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This update reflects the analyst's ongoing positive outlook on the IT services and consulting company, despite the minor price target revision. The article also provides a summary of recent news regarding CACI, including significant task order wins and an acquisition.

feed.title: Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) Sets New 1-Year High on Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) shares reached a new 52-week high after Wells Fargo raised its price target from $221 to $237 and maintained an "overweight" rating. Analysts generally hold a strong "Buy" rating for the stock, with a consensus price target of $216.33. The company recently reported an earnings beat, with EPS of $2.55 against estimated $2.22, and announced an annualized dividend of $2.04 with a 1.0% yield.

feed.title: Viavi Solutions Sees Unusually Large Options Volume (NASDAQ:VIAV)
feed.summary: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ:VIAV) experienced an unusually high volume of call options traded on Thursday, with 4,540 contracts, vastly exceeding the typical daily volume. This surge occurred as the stock price rose by 4.8%. The article also highlights significant insider selling totaling over $10.7 million in the past 90 days and details on institutional ownership, with 95.54% of the stock held by institutions.

feed.title: Via Renewables, Inc. Announces Dividend on Preferred Stock
feed.summary: Via Renewables, Inc. announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.65699 per share on its 8.75% Series A Fixed-to-Floating Rate Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock. The dividend will be paid on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026. This announcement also clarified that the replacement benchmark rate for the Series A Preferred Stock, following the end of Three-Month LIBOR, is Three-Month CME Term SOFR plus a tenor spread adjustment.

feed.title: AXT Target of Unusually High Options Trading (NASDAQ:AXTI)
feed.summary: AXT Inc. (NASDAQ:AXTI) experienced unusually high options trading with traders buying 3,089 call options, a 27% increase over the daily average, as its stock jumped 14.4% to $25.29. This comes despite significant insider selling by directors, who sold over 326,000 shares in the past three months. The company recently beat EPS estimates but analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average target price of $13.75.

feed.title: Adecoagro S.A. (AGRO): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Adecoagro S.A. (AGRO), highlighting it as a deep value opportunity despite financial distress. The company possesses undervalued farmland and industrial assets, operates a growing Brazilian sugar and ethanol business, and recently acquired Profertil to boost integrated operations. The successful execution of management actions to reduce leverage, control capital expenditure, and suspend dividends could lead to significant equity upside.

feed.title: Xcel Energy likely to shut off power this week due to fire danger in Colorado
feed.summary: Xcel Energy is considering shutting off power to approximately 9,000 customers in Larimer and Weld counties, including parts of Fort Collins, this week due to high fire danger. The potential outages are a preventative measure against dry and windy conditions. This decision reflects an ongoing concern for wildfire prevention in the region.

feed.title: Why data sovereignty matters more in the age of AI
feed.summary: This article features an interview with Thomas Saueressig of SAP, discussing why data sovereignty is increasingly critical, especially with the rise of AI. He explains that data sovereignty now extends beyond physical location to include control over operations, technology, and law, impacting how governments and organizations can responsibly adopt AI while maintaining security and compliance. SAP's "full-stack sovereignty" approach aims to address these challenges, offering solutions that embed control and compliance throughout the digital ecosystem.

feed.title: S&P Global Mobility launches new automotive data platform
feed.summary: S&P Global Mobility has launched Mobility Pulse 360, a new platform designed to integrate sales, dealer inventory, incentive programs, and pricing data for automakers into a single interface. This platform aims to help OEMs visualize market trends and competitor actions in real time, enabling faster and more informed decisions. The company, a division of S&P Global, developed this solution to address challenges like market volatility and the transition to electric vehicles, reinforcing its financial stability with a history of consistent dividend payments.
